title: Creating DNS zones without DNSSEC enabled is security-sensitive
description: DNSSEC (Domain Name System Security Extensions) protects DNS zones from spoofing and cache poisoning by cryptographically signing DNS data. Without DNSSEC, attackers can manipulate DNS responses, redirecting users to malicious sites or intercepting sensitive data.
message: DNS zone does not have DNSSEC enabled. Enable DNSSEC to protect against DNS spoofing.
impact: Unprotected DNS zones are vulnerable to spoofing and cache poisoning, leading to traffic interception and phishing attacks.
recommendation: Enable DNSSEC on all managed DNS zones. Regularly monitor DNSSEC status and renew keys as needed.

noncompliant.description: DNSSEC is disabled, leaving the zone vulnerable to spoofing.

compliant.description: DNSSEC is enabled, protecting the zone from spoofing and tampering.

devsecops.security: DNSSEC prevents attackers from redirecting traffic or intercepting sensitive data via DNS spoofing.
devsecops.automation: CI/CD should enforce DNSSEC for all managed zones.
devsecops.compliance: DNSSEC is recommended by NIST and required for some regulated environments.

why_is_this_an_issue: Without DNSSEC, DNS responses can be forged, leading to redirection, phishing, and data interception.
how_to_fix: Enable DNSSEC in the DNS zone configuration. Monitor for DNSSEC errors and renew keys as needed.

security_mappings.CERT: MSC12-C: Use DNSSEC to protect DNS integrity
